abstract: This research explores the integration of Islamic Education values within the Strengthening Pancasila Student Profile and Work Culture Project (P5BK) at SMKN 1 Masbagik to cultivate student character. Utilizing a qualitative descriptive approach, data were gathered through interviews, field observations, and documentation, and subsequently processed using the Miles and Huberman interactive analysis model. The findings reveal that Islamic values are embedded across three primary P5BK themes: sustainable lifestyles, employability, and local wisdom. The implementation process follows a structured four-stage framework consisting of planning, execution, evaluation, and the engagement of supporting factors. Results indicate that this integrated approach offers significant advantages, including instructional flexibility, strong synergy between the school and the industrial community, and the provision of authentic learning experiences that heighten environmental and social awareness. Theoretically, the study reinforces the role of the P5BK initiative within the "Merdeka Belajar" (Independent Learning) curriculum, suggesting that such integration enhances teacher pedagogical competence and elevates the overall quality of vocational education.
